commonIssues:
High incidence of driving without a license and minors driving two-wheelers/cars, resulting in accidents, police crackdowns, vehicle seizures, and heavy fines (₹500–₹1000 or imprisonment).

How to fix: Never allow minors to drive. Apply for a non-gear Learner's Licence at age 16 with parental consent, or wait until 18 for LMV. Keep a digital copy of your valid DL in DigiLocker at all times.

Many applicants, particularly youngsters, are failing the computerized driving tests, highlighting issues with training.

How to fix: Study the official traffic signs and rules handbook provided on the Parivahan portal before taking the online test. Take the mock tests available on the Sarathi portal to familiarize yourself with the question pattern.

Delays in receiving physical smart cards. Confusion caused by changes in DL numbering standards by the AP government, leaving records improperly updated.

How to fix: If your physical card is delayed, download your digital DL via the mParivahan or DigiLocker app, which is legally valid. If your DL number format is outdated, use the "Backlog Entry" service on AP e-Pragathi to digitize your old license.

Concerns over bypassing proper testing and resistance to private players issuing licenses due to fake license fears.

How to fix: Only take your test at official, technology-driven testing centers operated by the AP RTO. Do not pay intermediaries promising "guaranteed" passes without tests.
